TextENGNew: Grandmother's motorcycle had practically become my own legs by now.@k It was apparently better-performing and easier to ride than the one I actually owned.
Next up was the board meeting at the assembly hall inside Furude Shrine, starting in the evening.
changeReason: Original says 'the staff meeting'; new correctly says 'the board meeting' -- JPN 役員会. Also new adds 'starting in the evening' from 夕方から.

TextENGNew: It was easy to imagine what a normal board meeting would look like--one where they just discussed preparations for the Watanagashi festival.
changeReason: Original says 'celebrating the end of Watanagashi'; new correctly says 'discussed preparations for the Watanagashi festival' -- JPN 綿流しのお祭りを打ち合わせる.

TextENGNew: ...But today's board meeting would likely be every bit as tense as the Sonozaki main family's clan council.
changeReason: Original says 'staff meeting'; new correctly says 'board meeting' (consistent 役員会 correction).

TextENGNew: ...Yes, this board meeting was the innermost sanctum of Hinamizawa.
changeReason: Original says 'was going to deal with the heart of Hinamizawa'; new says 'was the innermost sanctum of Hinamizawa' -- being the sanctum vs dealing with it are different.

TextENGNew: Not even the slightest lapse in vigilance could be permitted...
I rested briefly at the main house to adjust my timing, then left.
I climbed the long stairway leading to Furude Shrine and entered the grounds.
Several village elders had gathered in front of the assembly hall, smoking cigarettes.
...Apparently there had been a small fire inside the hall long ago, so smoking had been banned indoors ever since.
``Oh, is that you, Mion-chan?``
changeReason: New adds 'the next head' from JPN 次期頭首, providing important political context about Mion's role.

TextENGNew: Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan, who also served as head of the Kimiyoshi family, noticed me and waved.
He apparently hadn't expected the next head, Mion, to come by herself.
He must have assumed that either Grandmother would come alone, or Mion would accompany her as an attendant.
``...Granny's been in a terrible mood ever since last night's incident.@k@vS06/06/150601760. ...She won't let anyone near her and has shut herself up in her futon.``
The old men seemed well aware of how difficult Grandmother became once she was in a bad mood.@k They smiled at me with sympathetic, wry grins.
``Then I take it Mion-chan is acting as the Sonozaki family head today?@k@vS06/18/153300027. Has Oryou-san told you anything...?``
``...More or less.``
Has Oryou-san told you anything?
I couldn't quite read what that question meant from that single remark alone.
The chairman of the board meeting was normally the village mayor--the head of the Kimiyoshi family.

And that very Kimiyoshi family head had just confirmed with the Sonozaki family's acting head whether she'd been properly briefed by the actual head...@k That made it painfully obvious who was truly meant to lead today's meeting.
``...Oji--...no, Kimiyoshi-san.@k@vS06/06/150601763. About today's meeting... do I have to chair it...?``
According to the hierarchy of the Sonozaki main family's clan council, the acting Sonozaki family head outranked the Kimiyoshi family head.
In other words, I would be today's chairman.@k ...There was no way I could do that.
``Oh, don't worry, don't worry. I'll handle it.@k@vS06/18/153300029. You just sit tight, Mion-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300030. If there's anything Oryou-san told you, just share it with us, all right?``
``Mayor, shall we get started? Everyone's here.``
The smokers ground out their cigarettes under their shoes and hurried back into the hall.
Low tables were arranged in a horseshoe shape, and I was invited to sit on the cushion next to the head seat.
The cushion on the opposite side of the head seat--my counterpart's position--was empty.
...Judging by its placement, it was probably the seat for the Furude family head, Rika Furude.
But someone had just said that everyone was here.
Either Rika-chama had already notified them of her absence, or the empty seat was purely ceremonial.
Given the subject matter of this meeting, it was only natural that even the current Furude family head would prefer not to attend.
...In truth, even if Rika-chama were present, there was nothing she could possibly contribute.
I knew that at the Sonozaki main family's clan councils, she always spent her time drawing pictures in a sketchbook.
Even if she attended this meeting, she would undoubtedly just sit there drawing.
Besides... the elders probably didn't want Rika-chama, the symbol of light, to hear about the village's darker affairs.
Thinking about it that way, the empty Furude family seat was easy to understand.
Once everyone was seated, the head of the women's auxiliary served tea.
...I caught a pointed, mother-in-law-ish look that said 'if you're a woman, help out,' so I 'voluntarily' (?) pitched in.
changeReason: New captures JPN nuance of '姑的な意地悪目線' (mother-in-law-ish mean look) and the ironic '(?)' about volunteering; original simplifies to 'evil look.'

TextENGNew: After that, once everyone had settled down and fallen silent, the board secretary, seated at the very lowest position, stood up.

``Well then, everyone, thank you very much for gathering on such short notice despite your busy schedules...@k@vS06/00/syoki15003. First, a few opening words from the mayor.@k@vS06/00/syoki15004. Mayor, if you please.``

``Ah, pardon me for remaining seated.@k@vS06/18/153300032. Ken-chan, you stay seated too.@k@vS06/18/153300033. ...Thank you all for coming this evening on a weekday.@k@vS06/18/153300034. And thank you for your hard work at Watanagashi yesterday.``

``Thanks to all of you, this year's festival concluded without incident and with great success.@k@vS06/18/153300036. People came from all over the village, and I believe it was truly a wonderful festival.``
``Ah, how many people came again?``
``According to the official count, approximately five thousand.``
Wa ha ha ha ha.
That was apparently meant to be funny--I was the only one who missed the cue to laugh.
``I hope we will continue to work together as one for the advancement of our culture.@k@vS06/18/153300040. ...Thank you for your continued support.``
Clap, clap, clap, clap, clap...
Applause from only about eight people sounded rather pathetic.

``Now then, let us proceed to today's agenda.@k@vS06/00/syoki15006. I'd like to ask Mayor Kimiyoshi to lead the proceedings.@k@vS06/00/syoki15007. Those in favor, please show your approval with a round of applause.``
Clap, clap, clap, clap!
``Thank you.@k@vS06/00/syoki15009. With unanimous approval, I will now turn the chair over to Mayor Kimiyoshi.@k@vS06/00/syoki15010. Please go ahead.``
``Thank you, Ken-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300042. Now then, I'll take over as chair and move into today's agenda...@k@vS06/18/153300043. Ah, sorry--shall we close the curtains?``
Everyone reached behind themselves to close the curtains on the windows at their backs.
Though it was still only evening, with the curtains blocking the sunlight, the room felt as dark as the dead of night...
First, the chairman explained the details of last night's incident and the interactions with the police.
...Late last night, a police vehicle returning to Okinomiya had discovered Tomitake-san's body.
Ooishi's special Oyashiro-sama's Curse task force (?) had been standing by in anticipation of a fifth year incident, so their initial response was extremely swift.
To track down Takano-san, who had always been seen with Tomitake-san, Ooishi's team contacted not only the prefectural police but neighboring prefectures as well.

As a result, they discovered that a report had come in about a woman's body being burned in a drum can in the mountains of the neighboring prefecture, and immediately confirmed the identity of the body.@k It was determined to be Miyo Takano.
Based on the circumstances, the police had classified this as the fifth installment of the Hinamizawa Village serial mysterious deaths, and the investigation was currently underway.
...When the factual explanation concluded, everyone fell into dead silence.
I, too, watched carefully to see who would speak next...
No one opened their mouth.
...Then I noticed Kimiyoshi-san looking at me.
Of course.@k ...This was where the Sonozaki family head--Grandmother--was supposed to speak first.
``Mion-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300045. ...Has Oryou-san said anything?``
All eyes turned to me.
...They were surely seeing Grandmother's figure standing behind me.
changeReason: Original says 'they could see the hag in me'; new says 'seeing Grandmother's figure standing behind me' -- different imagery entirely.

TextENGNew: ``...I am Mion Sonozaki, acting head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS06/06/150601765. I am attending today in place of our head, Oryou.@k@vS06/06/150601766. Thank you for having me...``
...I filled my mind with Mion.
changeReason: Original says 'I became Mion'; new says 'I filled my mind with Mion' -- JPN 頭の中を魅音で満たす; different levels of transformation.

TextENGNew: ...That terrifying presence from a year ago--those cold, unfeeling eyes that forced horrible things on people without flinching.
...Yes, that Mion existed within me too.
She was me.@k And I was her.@k ..................
``...Regrettably, Oyashiro-sama's wrath has descended for the fifth year as well.@k@vS06/06/150601768. ...This is deeply lamentable.``
Everyone sat in silence, hanging on my every word.
...That's right--I was no mere girl now.
Grandmother was speaking through a young girl's lips.
Awed by the weight of that authority, everyone simply listened in reverent silence...
changeReason: Original says 'trembled in fear'; new says 'reverent silence' -- JPN 畏怖 means awe/reverence; very different reactions.

TextENGNew: ``...Does anyone know why the curse struck again this year?@k@vS06/06/150601770. ...Anyone?``
At my question, everyone exchanged whispers with grim expressions.
``Precisely.@k@vS06/06/150601772. ...It is because they violated the sanctity of the ritual storehouse, trampling sacred ground with their filthy feet and bringing in defilement.``
````How could theyyyy!!````
Several old men bellowed and rose to their feet.
``Those imbeciles...!@k@vS06/00/eldA15003. Unbelievable brats!!``
``Even ripping out their guts wouldn't be enough for those little wretches!!``
changeReason: Original says 'They deserved to be killed!!'; new preserves the graphic JPN: 'Even ripping out their guts wouldn't be enough' (ハラワタ引き裂いても飽きたりん).

TextENGNew: ``Oyashiro-sama, please quell your anger... namu namu namu namu...``
``How could this happen, how could this happen...@k@vS06/00/eldD15002. ...Oyashiro-sama, Oyashiro-sama...``
``This is exactly why we can't stand outsiders!!``
``They don't think at all!!@k@vS06/00/eldE15003. They have no sense, none whatsoever!!``
``This is what happens to sacrilegious fools!! The blasphemers!!``
changeReason: Original frames it as 'deserved fate'; new frames it as 'religious condemnation' with 'sacrilegious fools' and 'blasphemers.'

TextENGNew: The outraged elders and the terrified elders all shouted over each other.
changeReason: Original conflates angry and fearful elders; new correctly separates them: 'The outraged elders and the terrified elders' -- matching JPN 憤る老人たち and 畏れ多さに慄く老人たち.

TextENGNew: The sheer intensity of their transformation filled me with no small amount of fear.

But what frightened me most was that even Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan, who had been kind to me since I was little and was always so gentle,@k wore the face of an asura as he cursed those who had violated the ritual storehouse.
The room had become a raging sea.
Roaring, swirling, crashing, spraying.@k Breaking, howling, snarling.
I sat alone, my face turning ashen as I watched the exchange...
Yesterday, Mion had threatened me. This morning, Kasai had been appalled.
And now, right before my eyes, the same sort of reality was unfolding.
Even the gentle Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan was loudly denouncing Takano-san and the others, shouting that they deserved to die, that even their manner of death was too lenient.
changeReason: New includes from JPN the detail that 'even their manner of death was too lenient' (死に方すらも生ぬるい), absent in original.

TextENGNew: I was frightened by the old men's sudden transformation.@k And their fanatic devotion--the conviction that anyone who violated Oyashiro-sama's ritual storehouse deserved death--was deviant and terrifying.
But... those were trivial matters.@k What truly scared me was something else entirely.
changeReason: Original says 'It wasn't just that'; new says 'those were trivial matters' -- opposite framing.

TextENGNew: Among the trespassers they were cursing, chanting 'die, die, be cursed and perish'... I was one of them.
In other words... they might as well have been hurling those curses of death directly at me...
If they discovered, right here and now, that I had snuck into the ritual storehouse...
...The thought that I might be strangled to death on the spot was not far-fetched in the least.
...In fact, given their ferocity, whether they would grant me a quick death was the more pressing concern.
changeReason: Original says 'they might not kill me that easily'; new says concern is 'whether they would grant me a quick death' -- different perspective.

TextENGNew: I was... certain of it.
These people were fanatical believers in Oyashiro-sama, sharing the same values as Grandmother.
They probably wouldn't hesitate to execute anyone who incurred Oyashiro-sama's wrath on his behalf.
Even if they didn't dirty their own hands directly, they would absolutely turn a blind eye.
Yes--even if I were strangled to death right here in front of them.
If everyone here conspired together and testified that I never attended today... that they waited but I never showed... it would be a completed 'demoning away.'
It would be all too easy to eliminate someone. To make them vanish.
There was no doubt about it.
......The people linking Grandmother to the curse were undoubtedly right here in this room...
...I pressed further.
Yes--I was Mion Sonozaki.
I was on the side that carried out the curse.
So right now, I had nothing to worry about.
...No one in existence could tell us twins apart.@k Therefore, I was safe...
``......Please, be quiet.``
``Hey, all of you!!@k@vS06/18/153300047. Quiet down, quiet down!!!``
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan backed up my call for order, but his tone carried an unbelievably violent roughness... more frightening than reassuring.
At any rate, everyone fell silent.
``...Four people broke into the ritual storehouse.@k@vS06/06/150601775. Two of them remain.``
The room stirred again, but Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan immediately barked at them and restored order.
``Tomitake-san and Takano-san were old enough to know better.@k@vS06/06/150601777. ...Oyashiro-sama's curse fell upon them immediately, but the other two are children.``
``The curse has yet to befall them... but they will not be forgiven without proper atonement.``
This time, no one made a sound.
...In fact, everyone had gone genuinely pale, desperately trying not to miss a single word I said.
Because they all feared that those two children might be... their own relatives... their own grandchildren...
...They wanted to know who they were as quickly as possible, to put their minds at ease.
``......The other two trespassers are... Shion Sonozaki.@k@vS06/06/150601780. ...And Keiichi Maebara.``
I could feel the air in the room drying out completely.
...So dry it seemed as though the very air might audibly crack...
``...Shion Sonozaki has...@k@vS06/06/150601782. ...already...``
From the silent elders, I couldn't hear so much as the sound of breathing.
I stole a glance at Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan sitting beside me.
...He had adored Shion as if she were his own granddaughter, yet she had already been 'demoned away.'
I wanted to see what kind of grief, or perhaps pity, would show on his face.
And when I saw his expression...
...I felt the skin on my back cracking and splitting, as though it could no longer withstand the bone-dry air.
............His expression was utterly blank.@k ...Devoid of emotion.
changeReason: Original says 'He didn't even have an expression'; new says 'His expression was utterly blank. Devoid of emotion' -- JPN 無機質, 無感情.

TextENGNew: Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan... had doted on me so much.
I loved him, and I thought he loved me too.
That was why I had hoped he would feel even the slightest pain in his heart upon learning of this cruel fate.
No--I had been naive.
The memory of his warm, gentle smile warped and distorted inside me.

...How can you possibly convey in mere thousands, tens of thousands of written characters the terror of someone you trusted, someone you wanted to be close to, suddenly transforming into a stranger...?@k You can't.@k Words could never convey this fear......
changeReason: New preserves the meta-narrative about the inadequacy of words to convey the terror; original dramatically simplifies.

TextENGNew: Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan noticed my gaze and spoke.
``......And the other one, Keiichi Maebara...?``
changeReason: New includes 'the other one' (もうひとりの) contextualizing Maebara; original omits.

TextENGNew: I had to steady my breathing to maintain my composure.
``...All four who defiled the ritual storehouse will suffer Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS06/06/150601785. ...Keiichi Maebara will be no exception...``
Cold eyes.@k Cruel eyes.@k Emotionless eyes.@k Eyes that cursed.@k And eyes ready to devour.
I could see the demons fixing their sharp gazes on me, chewing on my words as if savoring dried squid.
Some flicked their thin, serpentine tongues in and out.
Some had flames erupting from their eyes.
...They were etching into their minds the name of the trespasser the curse had not yet reached.
Eventually...
changeReason: Original says 'And so, the meeting went on'; new says 'Eventually...' -- JPN やがて means eventually, not a meeting continuation.

TextENGNew: ......Don't tell the police more than what they ask.
Report what questions they asked.
If anything further happens, contact us immediately and convene a board meeting.
If an unexpected situation requiring urgent judgment arises, seek the decision of the Kimiyoshi or Sonozaki family heads.@k With those points confirmed, the meeting was adjourned...
It was already dark outside.
Hinamizawa, nestled among mountains, grew dark quickly once the sun began to set in the evening.
Even if the daytime was hot, it rapidly cooled once darkness fell.
The elders exchanged brief farewells and departed on bicycles, motorcycles, or by car, each heading home...
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan, too, waved goodbye to the others and was about to set off for home.
...I called out to him.
``Kimiyoshi-san.@k@vS06/06/150601787. ...Do you have plans after this?``
Because I addressed him as 'Kimiyoshi-san' rather than 'Ojii-chan,'@k ...he stopped in his tracks.
``...No, nothing in particular.``
``Would you mind stopping by our house?``
``......Does Oryou-san... want to see me?``
``Yes.@k@vS06/06/150601790. ...She says there's something she'd like to discuss in person.``
``.........All right, I'll stop by.``
With just that brief exchange, Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan agreed to come to the Sonozaki main house.
Because Grandmother often summoned him for private discussions, I could bring him over with just this much conversation...
I showed Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan into the reception room.@k After preparing tea, I excused myself, claiming I would go fetch Grandmother, and killed some time around the corner of the hallway.
``...Granny says she'll come after she changes.@k@vS06/06/150601792. ...It might take a little while.``
``Oh, that's fine, that's fine.@k@vS06/18/153300053. I'll wait patiently.``
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan sipped his tea and relaxed, stretching out his legs.
...I sat down casually beside him.
``......I think... it's probably about Shion.``
I dropped my name into the conversation and watched for his reaction.
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan's expression... remained tense.
I couldn't even tell if he had heard me.
...I decided to keep talking until he responded.
``...There was that incident with Shion last year, you remember.@k@vS06/06/150601795. Getting close with someone from the Houjou family and all...``
``Last year... she was forgiven after pulling out her nails, wasn't she?``
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan folded his arms and sat in silence for some time.
``...Where is... Shion-chan now?``
``In the basement.``
``...How is she doing?``
His voice... sounded concerned.
Hearing that, I felt relieved... Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan was on my side after all.
His fierce demeanor at the assembly hall had just been for appearances...
``She's been crying the whole time.``
``...Mion-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300059. Tell me honestly--what does Oryou-san intend to do with Shion-chan?``
``She hasn't told me anything specific.@k@vS06/06/150601801. As you know, when Granny's anger exceeds a certain point, she actually goes quiet instead.``
``...She broke into the ritual storehouse, after all.@k@vS06/18/153300061. ...And it's her own flesh and blood, no less.@k@vS06/18/153300062. ...I can only imagine how torn she must be...``
He lowered his eyes with an expression suggesting that Grandmother must be agonizing over the decision.
``What do you think, Ojii-chan? ...What do you think will happen to Shion?``

``...Well... I'm sure it'll be far worse than just three nails this time.@k@vS06/18/153300064. Knowing Oryou-san, she won't go easy on someone just because they're family.@k@vS06/18/153300065. If anything, precisely because they're family... she'll be even harsher...``
``...After all... she did enter the ritual storehouse.@k@vS06/06/150601804. ...That's something that can never be forgiven...``
``......Indeed.@k@vS06/18/153300067. ...The ritual storehouse is...``
``But maybe Shion deserves some sympathy too.@k@vS06/06/150601806. ...After all, she's been kept away from Hinamizawa all this time.``
``...She may have known the ritual storehouse was off-limits, but... she probably didn't truly understand just how grave a transgression it was.``
``.........That's true. Poor Shion-chan...``

``Me or Shion.@k@vS06/06/150601809. Other than looking at the tattoo on my back, how could you possibly tell us apart?@k@vS06/06/150601810. ...You can't.@k@vS06/06/150601811. The distinction between Mion and Shion is utterly meaningless.``
Twins are an ill omen.
They must be dealt with as soon as they are born.
Following that old family precept, Grandmother actually put her hands around the newborn Shion's neck.@k Or so I had heard.
In the end, Grandmother didn't kill Shion... but treated her as though she didn't exist.
She forced the label of Mion on one of us and Shion on the other. She lavished love on Mion and was cold to Shion.
My sister and I never understood why we were treated that way.
Because to us sisters, it didn't matter which one was Mion and which was Shion.
So at some point... one of us suggested it.
The joys Mion received and the loneliness Shion endured--we would share them equally.
That was why we swapped places at every opportunity.
Both sisters were simultaneously Mion and simultaneously Shion.
The adults tried desperately to tell us apart, dressing us in different clothes and styling our hair differently.
But it was all futile.
No matter what markers they used to distinguish us, we could simply swap those too, and no one would ever know.
Eventually... they must have concluded that we kept swapping because we were always together.
There came a rare occasion when only Shion got to go to an amusement park.
............From that day on, we were torn apart.
``Mion-chan...``

``I can't even imagine... what will happen to Shion.@k@vS06/06/150601813. And whatever Granny decides... I'm in no position to object.@k@vS06/06/150601814. She's the type who refuses to listen to her own family's opinions.``
``...You're right.@k@vS06/18/153300071. ...That's always been one of Oryou-san's flaws...``
``...If Shion weren't Shion Sonozaki... she would have been killed long ago.``
``.........I can't begin to imagine... what kind of punishment would be considered equivalent to death.``
``...Even if she isn't killed... she'll probably never see the light of day again.@k@vS06/06/150601818. ...In other words... she'll be... 'demoned away'...``
``Mion-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300074. ...Shion-chan... does regret what she did, doesn't she?``
Then Ojii-chan tapped my shoulder gently, and slapped his own chest with a reassuring thump.
His smile was dependable.
``Don't worry.@k@vS06/18/153300076. If Shion-chan truly regrets it, there's no way she'll be demoned away.@k@vS06/18/153300077. Leave it to Ojii-chan.``

``Sure, Oryou-san will be furious if I speak up.@k@vS06/18/153300079. But I won't back down.@k@vS06/18/153300080. I'll tell her properly that Shion-chan truly regrets what she did, and beg her to forgive the girl.``
...A tear... spilled down my cheek.
I knew it... Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan was my ally after all.
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan alone said he would forgive Shion.@k He was on my side...@k I was so happy...
``Are you sure... it'll be all right?@k@vS06/06/150601822. She won't end up being demoned away, like Satoshi Houjou-kun was last year...?``
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan said it with a truly reassuring smile.
``A brat from the accursed Houjou family is one thing, but Shion-chan is a Sonozaki. Of course she'll be forgiven.``
``...A Houjou brat... you mean Satoshi-kun?@k@vS06/06/150601824. Last year's victim...?``
``The Houjou people had it coming.@k@vS06/18/153300084. Being demoned away is a possibility, sure, but that's only because they're Houjous.``
changeReason: Original presents 'demoning away' as certain fact; new presents it as possibility -- significantly different certainty level.

TextENGNew: ``Every last one of the Houjous deserves to be cursed.@k@vS06/18/153300086. Being demoned away is for people like them.@k@vS06/18/153300087. So Shion-chan will be fine.@k@vS06/18/153300088. She'll be just fine.``
``...Ojii-chan.@k@vS06/06/150601826. But... Shion atoned for Satoshi-kun last year, didn't she? ...Wasn't he supposed to be spared because of that...?``
``Atoned?@k@vS06/18/153300090. For what?``
changeReason: Original translates けじめ as 'Take care of that'; new correctly says 'Atoned' -- different meaning entirely.

TextENGNew: ``Remember... she pulled out three nails.@k@vS06/06/150601828. For Kasai-san, Uncle Yoshirou, and then for Satoshi-kun...@k@vS06/06/150601829. ...three nails for three people.``
``Eh?@k@vS06/18/153300092. Is that what the three nails were for?``
``Wasn't... that the meaning behind the three nails?``
``No... you're right.@k@vS06/18/153300094. ...There were three people involved, so three nails for atonement.@k@vS06/18/153300095. That's what I heard too.@k@vS06/18/153300096. ...But...``
``...Kasai-san, Yoshirou-san...``
``...and Shion-chan. That makes three.``
Wait... what?
I... endured such excruciating pain...?
Sure, they held me down so I couldn't thrash around.@k ...But I gritted my teeth and endured it...?
Because I had to endure until all three nails were pulled.
Because I didn't know which finger's nail was for Satoshi-kun.
Because what if Satoshi-kun's was the middle finger?
If I couldn't endure until the middle finger... Satoshi-kun wouldn't be forgiven.
That was why... I gritted my teeth and endured...?
But afterward, Mion came and told me, didn't she?
That everything had been forgiven.
...Huh?
...Huh... huh?
Mion said Satoshi-kun was forgiven... right?@k ...Didn't she?
I... heard it, didn't I?@k I'm sure I heard it.
...Didn't I?@k Eh?@k ...Eh?@k ...Huh?
......A chill.
...In a room where no air should have been moving... I felt a gust of cold wind.
That coldness... seemed to stroke the nape of my neck.
At that moment.@k ...My true self stood right behind me... and nodded.
My hair stirred though there was no wind... and the wickedness buried deep within my eyes opened its true gaze.
``...Why do you think Satoshi Houjou-kun was demoned away?``

``Satoshi-kun's parents certainly tried to sell out the village.@k@vS06/06/150601834. It was only natural that Oyashiro-sama's curse fell upon them.@k@vS06/06/150601835. But Satoshi-kun was merely their child.@k@vS06/06/150601836. ...What crime did he commit?``
``...Eh... Mion-chan?``

``I am asking what crime Satoshi-kun committed.@k@vS06/06/150601838. You just said that a brat from the Houjou family deserved to be demoned away.@k@vS06/06/150601839. I am asking you what crime warranted being demoned away.``
Kimiyoshi was flustered by the sudden interrogation, unsure how to respond.

``I don't need to hear it from you to know.@k@vS06/06/150601841. Oryou Sonozaki decided it.@k@vS06/06/150601842. She decided that Oyashiro-sama's curse would extend to everyone bearing the Houjou name.``
``W-...Wait, Mion-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300102. ...That's not... that's not how it was decided...``
``It wasn't decided that way?``
``No, nothing like that was ever decided...!@k@vS06/18/153300104. That the curse of Oyashiro-sama would eventually befall the Houjou family...``
``...We may have vaguely discussed something like that, but... no one ever stated it so explicitly. No one ever did...``
``Then on what basis did you just say 'a Houjou brat is one thing'?@k@vS06/06/150601845. What grounds did that statement have?``
``Ah... ah... I'm sorry, Mion-chan.@k@vS06/18/153300107. That was... I went too far. I apologize!``
``Exactly.@k@vS06/06/150601847. ...If they're Houjous, anything that happens is just the curse.``
``The notion that as long as someone bears the Houjou name, no one cares what happens to them--the spread of that mentality is the problem.``
``Whether Satoshi-kun was guilty or innocent, the name Houjou alone made him unclean.@k@vS06/06/150601850. The proliferation of that thinking is the entire problem.``

``And you were infected by that proliferating filth, contaminated by superstition and blind faith, unforgivable--someone like you is unforgivable--people like you cornered Satoshi-kun's family, ground them down until there was nothing left--poor Satoshi-kun, truly pitiful--what did he do? Nothing! He did nothing! He was simply born a Houjou! What crime is that? What punishment? What curse? What damnation was supposed to fall on him?! What crime did Satoshi-kun ever commit?!!!``
Gyaaaaaaahhhhhhhhh!!!
Kimiyoshi's upper body snapped backward as if struck, and he toppled over sideways.
That was that.
I pocketed my stun gun.
``...Sorry, Shion.@k@vS06/01/150100164. ...What did you just say?``

``Apparently Takano-san and Tomitake-san died last night.@k@vS06/06/150601853. Takano-san was found as a charred corpse.@k@vS06/06/150601854. Tomitake-san... seemed like a suicide.@k@vS06/06/150601855. ...I found out this morning.@k@vS06/06/150601856. ...I overheard my father talking about it on the phone with a relative...``
As I expected, Keiichi knew nothing about the incidents on the night of Watanagashi.
``Naturally the media isn't reporting it.@k@vS06/06/150601858. ...That's how it's always been.``
Keiichi stubbornly insisted that he couldn't believe it unless he saw it in the news.
But the investigations into the serial mysterious deaths were now classified as confidential.
Of course it wouldn't be reported.
``I hear the village mayor and several Sonozaki-connected politicians with gold badges put enormous pressure on the police.``
``Then you mean... the two deaths are... being covered up in secret?!``

``That's how it is.@k@vS06/06/150601861. ...Of course, the police are still investigating.@k@vS06/06/150601862. But since it's classified as a covert investigation, the investigation itself is apparently under severe restrictions.@k@vS06/06/150601863. You could essentially call this obstruction of the investigation.``
``...I can't believe it.@k@vS06/01/150100167. ...People died, and yet... it's being swept under the rug, dealt with in secret...``
``To put it more plainly...@k@vS06/06/150601865. ...it means that no matter who dies on the night of Watanagashi, it will never become public.``
``...Th-That's the most insane thing I've ever heard...!``
``Kei-chan.@k@vS06/06/150601867. ...Remember when I told you before that the villagers might be the ones carrying out Oyashiro-sama's curse?@k@vS06/06/150601868. ...That's exactly what I mean.``
``...At some point, a framework was created here in Hinamizawa... where every year, on the night of Watanagashi, someone can be killed in the name of Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
Yes.@k This was the truth behind the Hinamizawa Village serial mysterious deaths, commonly known as Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
On this date, under the pretext of Oyashiro-sama's curse, two people may be sacrificed.
These perpetrators use the curse as justification to kill one person and make another disappear.
When both of these axes align, Oyashiro-sama's curse occurs.
And this fertile soil was Hinamizawa itself... the very reason the curse existed.
Everything Kimiyoshi had told me was the truth.
The spreading conviction that enemies from the dam conflict deserved to die--that proliferating mentality was the very womb that gave birth to a faceless murderer.
Yes, it was the command system of the Sonozaki main family itself, created by Oryou--concealed behind a fog as murky as rice-washing water.
The most notable feature of the Sonozaki main family's chain of command was that the one giving orders and the one executing them were never connected by a visible line.
Oryou, the commander, never gave explicit orders.
She only needed to express her expectations.
And the clan council members surrounding her all harbored the desire to fulfill those expectations if given the chance.
They didn't need to be proactive about it.@k Passivity was enough.
But anyone who understood Oryou's will... when the opportunity arose, had the potential to become the executor.
Therefore, Oryou never knew who actually carried it out, and the relatives didn't know either.
But they all understood that things ultimately ended up exactly as Oryou wished, and they vaguely knew that one of their own had been responsible.
This passive cooperation system was meticulous, expansive enough to encompass the entire village, and its solidarity had been forged in the fires of the dam conflict, making it unbreakably strong.
Anyone could be the executor.
Therefore, everyone became a collaborator and an accessory.
If someone carried out the deed, others would seek to fulfill Oryou's expectations in different ways.
For example, by providing false alibis for the perpetrator, or passively obstructing investigations.
...Even without knowing who the actual culprit was, they would band together to protect whoever among their own had done it.
Yes.@k ...I could now say with certainty that I had unraveled the mechanism behind the Hinamizawa Village serial mysterious deaths, commonly known as Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
Let me organize it once more.
This system of eliminating two people unwanted by the village each year was built on three elements.
The first element: 'Regional Solidarity.'
In Hinamizawa, with its long history of insularity, this arose naturally.
It was refined through the dam conflict, becoming so thorough as to be called impenetrable.
This could be called a precondition of the land itself for the curse to occur.
Rather than something that could be created, it was better to think of the curse as arising where this element already existed.
Next: 'Shared Values.'
This element was crucial--without it, the curse could not occur.
Moreover, because Oryou Sonozaki herself cultivated this element, one could definitively say she established this system by her own will.
Sharing values meant clearly marking the enemy and making that designation a common understanding throughout the community.
No matter how united the village was, it would be meaningless if they didn't all share a common understanding of who the enemy was.
During the dam conflict, Oryou Sonozaki always designated enemies in clear, easily understood terms to facilitate solidarity.
Oppose the dam construction; the government is the enemy.@k ...But this was too vague.
A word like 'government' didn't tell anyone who to direct their hostility at.
But Oryou chose visible, concrete targets--the dam construction site was evil, its leader the site supervisor was evil.
This was easy to understand.
The villagers could treat everyone at the dam site as enemies and the site supervisor as the enemy commander.
Using the same method, she designated one enemy after another, labeling them and imprinting a catalogue of village enemies into every villager's heart.
Earlier, I called this 'infection.'
Those infected by it, and those who further spread it through the village, were the unwitting givers of murder orders.
When that infection spread throughout the village, when every person in Hinamizawa shared a common recognition of the enemy...
...That was when Hinamizawa as a whole became a single-willed 'executioner.'
Oryou combined these two elements--'Regional Solidarity' and 'Shared Values (the Enemy)'--@k designating enemies and imprinting them as enemies of the entire village, spreading the infection.
The law punishes both the one who commits the crime and the one who orders it.
But Oryou's cunning system didn't even require orders.
She only needed to 'feel troubled and concerned' about the pre-designated enemy.
But this alone wouldn't trigger the curse.
Oryou's concern was like a safety catch--even with the safety off, without a finger to pull the trigger, the curse still wouldn't fire.
The final necessary element: 'A Designated Day of Execution.'
That was the night of Watanagashi.
The night of the festival honoring Oyashiro-sama, guardian deity of Hinamizawa.
On this night, Oyashiro-sama's curse would strike, and two enemies of the village would vanish.
That was the shared understanding.
In other words, if an incident occurred on any of the other 364 nights of the year, it would be nothing more than a murder case.
...But a murder committed on this night alone would be elevated to 'Oyashiro-sama's Curse.'
To put it more plainly, they had spread the aberrant notion that on the night of Watanagashi, it was permissible to eliminate the village's enemies.
Every villager became a cell in the enormous organism called Hinamizawa, and each cell was taught who the enemy was.
Then, with the pre-designated date serving as an execution order, whichever cell happened to be in the best position to kill on that night would carry it out.
This was the true nature of Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
Therefore... whoever actually killed Satoshi-kun was not the true enemy.
The ones who built this murder system and designated Satoshi-kun's family as the enemy--they were the ones I had to avenge...!
...In the broadest sense, perhaps Hinamizawa itself--the very system of Oyashiro-sama's Curse--was the true enemy.
I had already disposed of Grandmother, the system's architect and ringleader.
Kimiyoshi, the foremost agent of spreading that system, was already in my hands.
What remained was to discover who else was involved, and to what extent.
...It was like digging up a weed's roots--painstakingly, tenaciously, probing deeper and deeper to ensure not a single root snapped off.
At the board meeting, I had announced that Keiichi Maebara was one of those who violated the ritual storehouse.
By now, it had surely become common knowledge throughout the village's underside.
That meant Keiichi Maebara had been marked as a target of Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
Takano-san and her companion had been discovered as gruesome corpses.@k This was Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
Then, under the pretext of appeasing the curse, two more people could be made to vanish through 'demoning away' as sacrifices.
The designated execution night of Watanagashi had already passed, but...@k just as with Satoshi-kun, 'demoning away' was allowed a certain window of time.
Within the next few days, some sinister hand would surely close in on Keiichi Maebara.
Carefully, so that my bait--Keiichi Maebara--wouldn't be snatched away... I would sink my fishing line deeper and deeper still...
Keiichi had been shouting over the phone for quite a while now.

He didn't want to go into the ritual storehouse. Takano-san invited him, I invited him, he only went in reluctantly.@k Being targeted for death over that was completely unreasonable--he yelled these pathetic complaints.
The more he swelled with crisis and tension, the more frantically he thrashed--and that would only make my fishing expedition more successful.
...After all, they say live bait gets more bites.
``What are you going to do about this?!?!``
changeReason: Original says 'Why the hell did you do that?!'; new says 'What are you going to do about this?!' -- JPN どうしてくれるんだよ means the latter.

TextENGNew: ``I had nothing to do with this!!!@k@vS06/01/150100171. What are you going to do?!@k@vS06/01/150100172. What the hell are you going to do?!@k@vS06/01/150100173. How are you going to take responsibility?!?!@k@vS06/01/150100174. Huh?!@k@vS06/01/150100175. Hey, are you listen--``
How annoying.
changeReason: Original says 'Shut up. I've heard enough of your pitiful tears'; new says 'How annoying' -- JPN うざい means annoying; original adds non-JPN content.

TextENGNew: I hung up the receiver, unilaterally ending my call with Keiichi.
It was pointless to say anything more to Keiichi while he'd lost his composure.
Besides, I had already accomplished my objectives of making Keiichi aware of the danger and putting him on guard.
After taking a moment to compose myself, I dialed the village mayor's residence.
``Hello!@k@vS06/00/villb15002. Kimiyoshi residence.``
``This is Sonozaki.@k@vS06/06/150601871. How is it going?@k@vS06/06/150601872. Has the mayor been found?``

``Oh, Mion-chan.@k@vS06/00/villb15004. ...We've called everywhere we can think of, but no luck.@k@vS06/00/villb15005. We can't find him...@k@vS06/00/villb15006. I don't know what to do...!@k@vS06/00/villb15007. Where on earth could he have gone...!``
``We've checked with everyone we could think of on our end too, but... nothing at all.``
``I consulted with Granny, and she says we should mobilize the youth group to conduct a search.``
``A-At this hour...?@k@vS06/00/villb15010. Besides, it's not certain he's actually gone missing...``

``It's right after Watanagashi, after all.@k@vS06/06/150601876. She thinks we should handle this cautiously.@k@vS06/06/150601877. If we still can't find him, we'll report it to the police first thing in the morning.@k@vS06/06/150601878. Whether he's found or not, it's better not to invite unnecessary scrutiny.``
``...Did Oryou-san say that?``
``Yes.@k@vS06/06/150601880. If you can't believe me without hearing it directly, I'll put her on the phone.``
``N-No...!@k@vS06/00/villb15013. I understand.@k@vS06/00/villb15014. We'll gather the youth group and search.@k@vS06/00/villb15015. If we can't find him, we'll contact the police in the morning.``
``Please handle the youth group phone tree.@k@vS06/06/150601882. I'll head over as well, representing Granny.``
``Th-Thank you.@k@vS06/00/villb15017. I'll get everyone together right away...``
``Good.@k@vS06/06/150601884. See you soon then.``
The youth group was summoned to Furude Shrine to search for the missing mayor.
As Grandmother's representative, I had to make an appearance as well.
Before heading out, I stopped by the underground secret room to taunt the mayor, telling him I was about to go search for him.
Incidentally, according to the label Mion had put on the keychain, this secret room was apparently called the 'Underground Ritual Storehouse.'

The man who had loudly proclaimed that those who violated the ritual storehouse deserved death was now, ironically, confined in a ritual storehouse himself--albeit an underground one--paying for his sins.
...I was probably the only person who found this irony amusing.
``How are you feeling, Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan?``
Clink...
Kimiyoshi couldn't speak, but he was able to signal that he'd noticed me by rattling his chains.

``...You know, right now the whole village is in an uproar because you've gone missing.@k@vS06/06/150601887. Your family contacted us, so I've arranged for the youth group to be mobilized for a search.@k@vS06/06/150601888. I'm on my way there now.``
It was the cruelest kind of taunt, of course.
But Kimiyoshi could only rattle his chains; expressing any emotion beyond an agonized grimace seemed beyond him.
``If you answer my questions properly, I'll ease things up a bit for you, okay?``
``You said earlier that you didn't know.@k@vS06/06/150601893. I'll ask you once more.@k@vS06/06/150601894. ...Please tell me everything you know about Satoshi Houjou-kun.``
``...Oh, sorry.@k@vS06/06/150601896. You want to talk but can't?@k@vS06/06/150601897. All right, I'll loosen things up just while we chat.``
``...I don't know, Shion-chan...@k@vS06/18/153300111. I truly don't know...``
``You don't even know if he's alive or dead?``
``...I truly don't know anything...``
``Well, I suppose that's how Hinamizawa's curse system maintains its secrecy.@k@vS06/06/150601900. It's not impossible that you genuinely don't know.``
``Then let me rephrase the question.@k@vS06/06/150601902. As head of the Kimiyoshi family, I'd like to hear your opinion on what became of Satoshi-kun.``
``...Is Satoshi-kun... alive?@k@vS06/06/150601904. Or dead?``
I had only asked him to give his opinion as Kimiyoshi family head.
An opinion can be voiced even without knowledge of the facts.
Kimiyoshi was clearly struggling over how to answer.
What could he say that wouldn't displease me...?
It was plainly obvious he was trying to figure out how to appease me.
``...............I've heard a rumor... that he might still be alive...``
``Ah, the one about him taking the bullet train to Tokyo?``
``...The police... apparently spotted him in Tokyo... something along those lines...``
I'd heard from Ooishi that someone saw Satoshi-kun boarding a Tokyo-bound bullet train at Nagoya Station.
But including the veracity of that information, the police had found no further trace of Satoshi-kun after that.
So this was nothing more than Kimiyoshi making things up.

``...Say, Head of the Kimiyoshi family?@k@vS06/06/150601907. If you've gone to the trouble of such an elaborate disappearing act for a member of a cursed family, what possible reason would there be to keep him alive?@k@vS06/06/150601908. There wouldn't be one, would there?``
``Besides, please tell me the source of this 'police sighting in Tokyo.'@k@vS06/06/150601910. I was in close contact with Ooishi for a period, and I never heard anything like that.``
``...Ojii-chan?@k@vS06/06/150601912. If you're making things up to placate me... that's only going to make me angrier, you know?``
``...S-Sorry...!!@k@vS06/18/153300118. I'm not trying to make you angry, Shion-chan... I'm sorry...!``
``I'm not interested in hearing things meant to appease me.@k@vS06/06/150601914. I'm simply asking for Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan's cooperation in investigating what happened to Satoshi-kun.``
``I-I... understand... I'll think about it seriously...!``
...Seeing that I had eased up, Kimiyoshi began groaning loudly and visibly straining to think, as if to demonstrate that he was taking the question seriously.
``Straight answer.@k@vS06/06/150601916. ...Do you think he's alive?@k@vS06/06/150601917. Or do you think he's dead?``
``...............I think... it would be difficult to believe... he's alive...``
``......@k@vS06/06/150601919. ...I thought so.@k@vS06/06/150601920. There's no point in bearing the enormous risk of keeping him alive, is there.@k@vS06/06/150601921. I agree with you.``
I hadn't meant anything particularly deep by it, but my words seemed to terrify Kimiyoshi even further.
``The police believed Satoshi-kun murdered his aunt, and that he fled using the money he'd been saving for Satoko's birthday present.@k@vS06/06/150601923. ...What do you think about that?``
``...I... ahaha, maybe it's strange for me to say this, but I believe Satoshi-kun did kill his aunt.``
``That addict they arrested was probably a decoy arranged by Grandmother, don't you think?``
``......I'd suspected as much.@k@vS06/18/153300123. ...That person appeared out of nowhere and confessed.@k@vS06/18/153300124. ...It never sat right with me.``
``...So he was a decoy after all?``
``...I believe so...``
``Why do you think that was done?``
``...The police were investigating Satoshi-kun as their prime suspect, weren't they?``
``If they wanted Satoshi-kun to remain 'demoned away,'@k@vS06/18/153300128. ...having the police investigation drag on indefinitely must have been... inconvenient, I'd imagine...``
Kimiyoshi's opinion was interesting.
...He was absolutely right.
If the police kept investigating with Satoshi-kun as their suspect, it would create problems for whoever had demoned him away.
So the theory that a decoy was fabricated to end the investigation carried considerable weight.
``But fabricating a culprit isn't something just anyone can do, is it?``
``.........The only person capable of that sort of thing...@k@vS06/18/153300130. ...is Oryou-san.``
``...I knew Grandmother wielded considerable power in the underworld, but...@k@vS06/06/150601931. I didn't realize it went that far.``
``...I'm sure you've heard all sorts of rumors from the dam conflict era. And haven't some of those been things you heard directly?``
During the dam protests, I actually witnessed them issuing orders to relatives--@k destroy their heavy machinery,@k set up roadblocks to obstruct construction vehicles--things like that.
And when their comrades were arrested by the police, they'd get them released, create alibis, destroy evidence, or fabricate it.
I had indeed seen them giving such instructions to their inner circle,@k and I'd seen Grandmother summoning legislators to give them directives or lodge protests.
...As a child, I'd simply thought, 'Grandmother is really something.'
But looking back now, it was far more than 'really something.'
...She was literally the mastermind pulling the strings of the underworld.

``Shion-chan, you've heard about the kidnapping of the Ministry of Construction executive's son... no, the grandson of the Minister of Construction.@k@vS06/18/153300133. You know about that case, right...?``
``So that really was a kidnapping?@k@vS06/06/150601933. I knew the grandson was 'demoned away' and then found deep in the mountains of Takatsudo or Yagouchi, but...``

``...That was on Oryou-san's orders...@k@vS06/18/153300135. ...The Minister promised to halt the dam construction, so the grandson was released. All the senior members of the former Alliance know this.``

``...Hmm.@k@vS06/06/150601935. ...I guess because I was too close to it all, I never fully grasped the scope.@k@vS06/06/150601936. ...Grandmother, the Three Families, the former Alliance... they really were something else.@k@vS06/06/150601937. ...How terrifying.``
...I was somewhat pleased that Kimiyoshi understood his situation and was properly answering my questions.
...But with each convincing thing Kimiyoshi said, the immovable reality that Satoshi-kun was no longer alive grew more certain... and that filled me with sorrow.
``...Who do you think... actually carried out the demoning away of Satoshi-kun?``
``...I don't know...@k@vS06/18/153300137. Besides, it was an unwritten rule never to ask who actually did it...``
Of course.
That was how the system worked.@k ...My question had been poorly framed.
``Regardless of who did it... it was quite impressive, wasn't it.@k@vS06/06/150601940. After all, killing someone and leaving no body to be found--that's no easy feat.``

``...Setting aside who killed him...@k@vS06/18/153300139. ...For instance, it's conceivable that Oryou-san assisted with disposing of the body.@k@vS06/18/153300140. ...You know about the well further back from here, the one used for dumping bodies, don't you...?``
``Ah... you're right.@k@vS06/06/150601942. Dumping a body in the well here would certainly be far safer than burying it somewhere in the mountains.``
The missing person is processed as having been offered as a sacrifice to appease Oyashiro-sama's wrath.
Those are the rules of this game.
Therefore, it would be inconvenient for the body to be discovered within the narrative of the curse.
That's precisely why missing persons' bodies must be disposed of somewhere they'll never be found.@k It was an entirely logical conclusion.
Then... Satoshi-kun is... at the bottom... of that well?
I grabbed a flashlight and headed toward the great cavern with the stone cells.
I turned on the lights in the cavern, and Mion in her cell noticed my arrival, fixing me with an anxious gaze.
I ignored her and headed for the cell containing the hidden well.
...I had to admire how naturally it was camouflaged; the concealment was almost artistic.
I aimed the flashlight down into the well.
Its depth was unfathomable. The feeble beam of light could not overcome the darkness pooling and stagnating at the bottom.
I... drew in a deep breath.@k ...And screamed into the well.
Satoshi-kun's name echoed deep, deep, deeper, reverberating again and again.@k ...There was no question the well was extraordinarily deep.
I considered climbing down using the ladder-like wedges driven into the wall.
But with the flashlight dangling from my neck, I couldn't properly see where to place my hands.@k And even with adequate lighting, whether the wedges were securely driven in was questionable at best.
Descending into this well seemed like nothing short of suicide.
...Satoshi-kun was... at the bottom of this...?
And then.
...A warm hand... unmistakably... stroked my head.
Shion.@k ...You finally... found me...
Satoshi-kun... Satoshi-kun... Satoshi-kun...!!
I... you know?!@k I've been searching for you... all this time...@k But... I couldn't find you while you were still alive...@k I'm sorry... I'm sorry...
...It's not your fault, Shion.@k@r ...There was nothing you could have done@r before I was killed.@k@r But... after I was gone,@r you did everything you possibly could...?
Satoshi-kun,@k ...I'm coming to you.@k Right now.@k I'll jump, so... please catch me.
...Mmph.
...Satoshi-kun said it with a rueful smile.
I immediately understood it was a refusal, and it made me sad.
Shion, just you finding me... makes me so happy.
...But Shion, you're alive.@k@r Being alive is so important...@r ...it's not something you should waste.
If you're not here, then being alive doesn't matter...!

I want to be with you, Satoshi-kun...@k I want to be together...@k I want you to stroke my head again... I want to spend time with you... this time, I'll be an honest, sweet girl...... I want... to be with you... forever...
Live.
...Do I have to live...?
Live, and fill your life with happiness, okay?
Living alone... what's the point...?
You'll find that out, as long as you keep living...
The well that had seemed so eerie just moments ago... somehow felt warm now.
I wasn't allowed to descend to the bottom.
...But the rim of this well... was the closest I could be to Satoshi-kun...
``...Mion.@k@vS06/06/150601948. ...I found Satoshi-kun......@k@vS06/06/150601949. He's at the bottom of the well... isn't he?``
``............I think so...@k@vS06/03/150300400. ...There's nowhere else... he could be.``
Having said just that... Mion's eyes welled up with tears, and she began to sob.
``...You weren't there... when Satoshi-kun was killed, were you?``
``If you had been.@k@vS06/06/150601952. ...Would you have begged Grandmother to spare him...?``
``Absolutely.@k@vS06/03/150300403. At the very least, I would have begged for his life...``
``Liar.``
``LIAAAAARRRRR!!!``
That day, one year ago.
...I vividly recalled how cruel Mion had been--cold, ruthless, every bit the future head--when she forced me to tear out my nails as atonement.
Even if she had been present when Satoshi-kun was killed, she absolutely would never have removed that cold mask of the next family head.
Brought to this nightmarish torture chamber...@k Satoshi-kun, who must have been terrified beyond words, would have seen Mion's familiar face and felt such desperate hope for salvation.

He must have believed Mion's cold expression was only an act, forced upon her in front of the relatives...

He must have believed she would help him any moment now... believed, and believed... but she never did... as he was tightened further and further into those terrible restraints... and still, she didn't help.
